title: Graf Yonna

{{Quote|The list of operatives that Graf has rescued would fill a datapad. Thousands of Rebels are alive today because of his actions. Unfortunately, Graf also tends to act prematurely.|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]]|<ref name="CRO" />}}
'''Graf Yonna''' was a native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bront]] and fought with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] around [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|A trio of Rebel operatives were attempting to lure an Imperial general into a trap. As part of the lure, the operatives had to appear to be almost overcome by Imperial forces. Just as the general approached, Graf Yonna appeared and started blasting away, killing the general. Graf's only response was "I acted against evil."|Airen Cracken|<ref name="CRO" />}}
Graf Yonna was born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bront]] around [[35 BBY/Legends|35 BBY]]. He witnessed the atrocities of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] when they [[Destruction of Bront|destroyed]] his homeworld and killed his family. He suffered a mental break and assumed the identity of [[Parsol d'Ahndole]], an adventurer and hero from the time of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

He joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and became a top operative for them as he would charge recklessly into battle and had saved countless Rebel lives. On [[Baes Logia]] the Rebels [[Baes Logia campaign|attempted]] to trap an Imperial [[General/Legends|General]] but Yonna arrived too early and ended up killing the general without hesitation or remorse.<ref name="CRO" />

==Personality and traits==
Yonna was driven insane when the Empire destroyed his family and home. He developed an [[Multiple personality disorder|alternate personality]] modeled after a Republic hero's romanticized persona. In particular, this personality exhibited by Yonna was melodramatic, had an extreme intolerance toward those he perceived to be evil and drove him to perform heroic feats.

Alliance psychologists attempted to restore Yonna's original personality to a moderate degree of success. When he was in his 'Graf' personality he was prone to fits of depression and drunkenness, anger, and rage all directed towards the Empire.<ref name="CRO" />

==Skills and abilities==
Despite his willingness to charge head first into danger, Yonna was not too skilled in combat. He was decent with his fists and with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] but his tremendous success in helping the Rebels would appear to be more with dumb luck and the element of surprise then overwhelming skill and tactics.<ref name="CRO" />

==Equipment==
Yonna was a walking arsenal and would carry a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]], [[Light repeating blaster/Legends|light repeating blaster]], a [[Sniper rifle/Legends|sniper rifle]] with [[Macrobinoculars/Legends|macrobinocular]] interface, a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]], two [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]], a [[Blast vest/Legends|blast vest]] and macrobinoculars with a headstrap for hands free and night vision operations.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Graf Yonna was designed by [[Chuck Truett]] for ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] in [[1994]]. He was illustrated by [[Mike Jackson]].
